rationale_for_baseline_tier: Painless, localised, non-scarring hair loss in a systemically well adult is a low-acuity dermatological problem. Routine GP review within a week for examination and screening bloods is appropriate. No feature warrants same-day or emergency assessment; the intense anxiety is not itself an escalation trigger.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Scalp becomes red, scaly, crusted or painful, or the patch spreads rapidly with broken hairs
escalation_edges.rationale: These features suggest infectious tinea capitis or a kerion needing antifungal treatment and contact precautions.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the bald area becomes red, flaky, crusted or sore, or spreads quickly, see your GP within a day or two — it may need treatment for a fungal infection.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: New fever, widespread rash, joint pains, or mouth ulcers
escalation_edges.rationale: Systemic features would raise concern for a systemic autoimmune process (e.g. lupus) requiring further assessment.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you develop fevers, a rash elsewhere, joint pains or mouth ulcers, book to see your GP promptly.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is very likely a benign, non-contagious condition. Book a routine appointment with your regular GP within the next week for an examination and some screening blood tests. There is no need for emergency care.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: No after-hours care is needed for this. If you are very distressed, healthdirect (1800 022 222) can provide reassurance overnight.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Telehealth impression of focal non-scarring alopecia (likely alopecia areata); requests GP examination, tinea exclusion, and thyroid/iron screening.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Dismissing entirely without any danger-mimic screening or follow-up could miss infectious tinea or early systemic disease.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An unrecognised tinea capitis spreads to household contacts and causes scarring.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Not applicable — this is a low-acuity presentation.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Sending a painless localised patch in a well patient to urgent care or the ED wastes resources and amplifies health anxiety.
